articles.title: Belgium vs Senegal FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 Live Score: Romelu Lukaku, Nicolas Jackson Benched; Check Line Ups
articles.description: FIFA World Cup 2026 Belgium vs Senegal Round of 32 Live Score: Kevin de Bruyne and Sadio Mane will have the eyes of the world as they go head-to-head in what is likely their final World Cup as Belgium and Senegal face each other at the Seattle Stadium. Line Ups Belgium Starting XI GK: Thibaut Courtois DEF: Arthur Theate, Brandon Mechele, Maxim De Cuyper, Timothy Castagne MID: Kevin De Bruyne (C), Youri Tielemans, Hans Vanaken ATT: Leandro Trossard, Jeremy Doku, Charles De Ketelaere Substitutes: Senne Lammens, Mike Penders, Thomas Meunier, Koni De Winter, Joaquin Seys, Nathan Ngoy, Axel Witsel, Diego Moreira, Alexis Saelemaekers, Nicolas Raskin, Amadou Onana, Romelu Lukaku, Dodi Lukebakio, Matias Fernandez-Pardo. Senegal Starting XI GK: Mory Diaw DEF: Ismail Jakobs, Krepin Diatta, Moussa Niakhaté MID: Idrissa Gana Gueye (C), Pathé Ciss, Habib Diarra, Pape Gueye ATT: Sadio Mané, Iliman Ndiaye, Ismaila Sarr Substitutes: Yehvann Diouf, Mamadou Sarr, Kalidou Koulibaly, Abdoulaye Seck, Antoine Mendy, El Hadji Malick Diouf, Lamine Camara, Pape Matar Sarr, Bara Sapoko Ndiaye, Assane Diao, Bamba Dieng, Nicolas Jackson, Cherif Ndiaye, Ibrahim Mbaye. Senegal will aim to reach the Round of 16 for only the third time in history, while Belgium can reach this stage for the seventh time. This is the first time in history the two teams are playing each other in a senior international men's match

articles.title: 'It's all about me, not rivals': Rehan Ahmed says Shane Warne, Adil Rashid shaped his approach
articles.description: Rehan Ahmed, England's rising star in spin bowling, is ready to step into a larger role, leveraging the wisdom shared by veterans Adil Rashid and the late Shane Warne. With enhanced confidence compared to the last Test series, Ahmed looks forward to competing against India's best batsmen, emphasising personal growth rather than merely analysing opponents. He also credits Ben Stokes' leadership for inspiring the team's dynamic approach.

articles.description: FIFA defended the controversial VAR decision that disallowed Germany's extra-time goal against Paraguay, saying that officials were told to punish attackers who deliberately try to obstruct opponents without an attempt to play the ball.

articles.title: DR Congo manager’s tactics may provide tougher challenge for England than Opta stats suggest
articles.description: Despite the chances of victory in normal time being 73.9%, Sébastien Desabre has changed his approach for stronger teams such as England England head into their match against the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) expecting to win. Opta rates their chances of victory in normal time at 73.9%. Yet the tactics deployed by the DRC manager, Sébastien Desabre, will make it harder than this probability suggests. Desabre has a clear strategy for facing the stronger teams in this tournament that differs from how he approached qualifying matches. The DRC used a four-man defence in 12 of their 13 games in qualification, retaining a back four in their pre-tournament friendly against Chile. They switched to a back five for a warm-up match with Denmark, using the same 5-3-2 formation for their group stage games with Portugal and Colombia . A DRC team ranked 65th in the world held sides rated 19th, sixth and 17th respectively to just two goals across these three games. Continue reading...

articles.title: Penalty shootouts: how Australia can avoid the cruellest World Cup fate | Jack Snape
articles.description: The Socceroos have never gone to spot kicks in a men’s World Cup but can turn to strategies as they prepare for the last-32 clash with Egypt ⚽️ How and where to watch the Socceroos games in Australia ⚽️ Full Socceroos coverage | Player guide Germany know it . So do the Netherlands . The pain of the penalty shootout is the cruellest fate in football, the individual spotlight on the kickers serving only to embarrass those who fail the sport’s most basic test. Australians understand the stakes well too. Though the Socceroos have never faced a shootout in a World Cup, the most replayed moment in the history of men’s football in Australia is John Aloisi’s penalty to defeat Uruguay in the World Cup playoff in 2005. The memory of Cortnee Vine’s 2023 winner for the Matildas against France perhaps exceeds even Aloisi’s spot kick. Continue reading...
